thread.title: SC attorney general, 23 others asks Supreme Court to limit girls' sports to biological females
thread.title_full: SC attorney general, 23 others asks Supreme Court to limit girls' sports to biological females

author: @foxcarolinanews
title: SC attorney general, 23 others asks Supreme Court to limit girls' sports to biological females
text: COLUMBIA, S.C. (FOX Carolina) - South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson is joining 23 other states in asking the U.S. Supreme Court to limit girl’s sports teams to biological females. The attorneys general want the highest court in the country to hear the case on Arizona’s law after a federal appeals court ruled the law likelye violates the Constitution’s Equal Protection Clause. In a brief to the Supreme Court, they argue that their states also have laws or policies like Arizona’s that restrict girls' sports teams to biological females. “Basing the distinction on biology rather than gender identity makes sense because it is the differences in biology—not gender identity—that call for separate teams in the first place: Whatever their gender identity, biological males are, on average, stronger and faster than biological females. If those average physical differences did not matter, there would be no need to segregate sports teams at all." They are asking the Supreme Court to hear the case and reverse the lower court’s ruling, to make it clear that the Constitution does not prohibit states from saving women’s sports from unfair compettition and providing meaningul athletic opportunities for girls and women.
